scope: Versioning the WHOLE deployable LLM-app artifact (prompts + compiled programs + model pins + retrieval config + eval-set version) as one bundle so a deploy is reproducible and rollback-able. Broader sibling of per-model-artifacts (which versions one compiled prompt); this overlay versions everything that ships together.
core_distinction: An LLM app artifact is not an ML model. An ML model = one weights file. An LLM app artifact = a manifest of heterogeneous, separately-mutable components (prompt text, compiled program JSON, model snapshot id, retriever config, embedding-bound index pointer, eval-set hash) any one of which silently changes prod behavior.

operations.name: ArtifactManifest
operations.trigger: Preparing to deploy an LLM app, or asking 'what exactly is running in prod right now?'
operations.action: Enumerate every behavior-affecting component and write a single manifest.json (or deploy.lock) that pins each: prompt file hashes, compiled-program path+sha, model snapshot id per call site, retrieval config (embed model, index pointer, top_k, reranker), eval-set version hash, framework versions. The manifest IS the deployable unit, not any single file.
operations.output: deploy/manifest.<version>.json — one bundle id covering all components; grep-able answer to 'what is in prod'.
operations.evidence: per-model-artifacts treats one compiled prompt as a (program x LM x dataset) triple; this overlay extends the same discipline to the full bundle. MLflow Model Registry versions a model + signature + run; here the 'model' is a composite app.

operations.name: ModelSnapshotPinning
operations.trigger: Any call site that names a model; CI sees an alias pin
operations.action: Pin dated snapshots everywhere (gpt-4o-2024-08-06, claude-3-7-sonnet-20250219), never moving aliases (gpt-4o, claude-3-5-sonnet, latest). Record each call site's model in the manifest. Cross-link [[per-model-artifacts]] OP-2 for the per-prompt detail.
operations.output: manifest.models[] list with provider/snapshot per call site; no 'latest' anywhere.
operations.evidence: per-model-artifacts SKILL section 2 claim 2: 'the snapshot, not the alias, is the identity'; Anthropic does not roll aliases, OpenAI does — both bite silently.

operations.name: ConfigAsCode
operations.trigger: Retrieval/runtime config lives in a dashboard, env var, or notebook cell
operations.action: Move every behavior knob into version-controlled config (top_k, chunk size, reranker on/off, temperature, system-prompt path, index pointer). The manifest references config by commit/hash. Dashboards are not artifacts.
operations.output: config/ tree under git; manifest references config_sha; nothing behavioral lives outside VCS.
operations.evidence: per-model-artifacts cross-framework: Aider edit-format defaults and LlamaIndex Settings are config artifacts too — 'artifact generalizes beyond compiled JSON'.

operations.name: EvalSetLinkage
operations.trigger: Bundling a version; about to tag a release
operations.action: Tie the bundle to the exact eval-set version (sha256 of canonical eval bytes) that produced its scores. A bundle without a pinned eval-set has no reproducible score. Cross-link [[regression-gate]]: the gate re-runs THIS eval-set on the new bundle vs parent.
operations.output: manifest.eval_set_sha + scores; reproducible 'this bundle scored X on eval-set Y'.
operations.evidence: dspy-sop Stage 2: 30 min / 300 recommended dev set; score only meaningful relative to a fixed set. per-model-artifacts ties dataset_sha256_8 into artifact identity.

operations.name: BundleAndVersion
operations.trigger: All components pinned; ready to ship
operations.action: Assign one monotonic bundle version (deploy/v<n> or git tag deploy/<n>) covering the whole manifest. Components may have independent versions internally, but the BUNDLE has one id that production deploys atomically.
operations.output: git tag deploy/v7 -> resolves to manifest.v7.json -> resolves all component versions.
operations.evidence: per-model-artifacts OP-10 GitTagOnRelease (per-prompt); this elevates to a whole-bundle tag. MLflow Stage promotion analogy.

operations.name: RollbackRecipe
operations.trigger: Prod incident; need to revert to last known-good behavior
operations.action: Deploy reads the bundle tag, not HEAD. Rollback = re-point the deploy to the previous bundle tag (git checkout deploy/v6). Because the bundle pins ALL components, rollback restores prompts + model + config + index pointer together — no partial revert.
operations.output: One-command atomic rollback: deploy deploy/v6. No 'oops the prompt reverted but the model didn't'.
operations.evidence: per-model-artifacts OP-10: 'Deploy reads the tag, not main HEAD. Atomic rollback via git checkout <tag>.' This overlay makes the unit the whole bundle.

operations.name: ComponentDriftAudit
operations.action: Diff manifest vN vs vN-1 field-by-field: prompt_sha changed -> prompt drift; model snapshot changed -> model drift; config_sha changed -> config drift; eval_set_sha changed -> you compared apples to oranges; index pointer changed -> retrieval drift. The manifest makes the change surface explicit.
operations.output: Root-cause class from a manifest diff, before re-running anything.
operations.evidence: per-model-artifacts section 4.4 lineage ops: diff REGISTRY entries to classify dataset/config/framework/environment drift.

operations.name: RetrievalConfigBinding
operations.trigger: App uses RAG; index built with a specific embedding model
operations.action: Pin the index pointer (which index dir) AND its embedding model + dim in the manifest. Loader refuses to start if runtime embed model != manifest embed model. The index is part of the deployable bundle even though it lives elsewhere.
operations.output: manifest.retrieval = {index_path, embed_model, dim, corpus_sha8, top_k, reranker}.
operations.evidence: per-model-artifacts Case C and cross-framework: embedding model is part of index identity; mixing spaces is the worst-case silent failure.

notes: SKILL section 4 surfaces 5 ops as required by spec (artifact manifest, model-snapshot pinning, config-as-code, rollback recipe, eval-set linkage). OP-7/OP-8 included as supporting ops referenced in dilemma cases and anti-patterns.
